---
title: "3 datasets combining"
author: "YC"
date: "2026-06-22"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
## data preprocessing-standard beta value matrix and pd group information
### GSE199057
```{r GSE199057}
## GSE199057 EPIC methylation processed matrix pipeline
##   1. Read GEO metadata.
##   2. Build phenotype table pD.
##   3. Extract AVG_Beta columns from the processed matrix.
##   4. Map processed sample IDs to GSM IDs.
##   5. Remove plate-control samples.
##   6. Generate a strict beta matrix: CpG probes × GSM samples.
##   7. Remove probes with missing beta values.
##   8. Align pD rows with beta matrix columns.
##   9. Run ChAMP filtering.

############################################################
## Module 0. Clear environment and load required packages
############################################################
rm(list = ls())
options(stringsAsFactors = F)
require(GEOquery)
require(Biobase)
library("impute")
library(ChAMP)

############################################################
## Module 1. Read GEO metadata
############################################################
# Ensure that the methylation beta matrix and the phenotype information strictly correspond to each other
# Download/read GEO series matrix metadata.
eset <- getGEO("GSE199057",destdir = './',AnnotGPL = T,getGPL = F)
#beta.m <- exprs(eset[[1]])

############################################################
## Module 2. Build phenotype table pD
############################################################
## Build a clean phenotype table.
## ID is the GSM accession.
## title is the original GEO sample description.
## pD.all contains sample-level information, including:
##   - GSM accession IDs
##   - sample titles
##   - clinical/sample descriptions
## Modify this part when applying the pipeline to another dataset
pD.all <- pData(eset[[1]])
pD <- pD.all[,c(1,2)]
names(pD) <- c("group", "ID")
pD$group <- ifelse(
  grepl("normal",pD$group,ignore.case = T),
  "Normal","Tumor")
pd <-pD
#⭐Check if the extracted groupings are correct
check_df <- data.frame(Title = pD.all$title, Group = pD$group)
head(check_df, 10)
tail(check_df,10)
table(pd$group)
############################################################
## Module 3. Read processed methylation matrix
############################################################

## Read the processed methylation matrix.
## For GSE199057, this matrix contains:
##   TargetID
##   sample_id.AVG_Beta
##   sample_id.Detection Pval
#beta.m <- exprs(eset[[1]])
library(data.table)
beta.m <- fread("GSE199057_processed_methlyation_matrix.txt.gz",data.table = F)
dim(beta.m)
############################################################
## Module 4. Extract AVG_Beta columns
############################################################

## Identify columns containing beta values.
## These are the columns ending with ".AVG_Beta".
beta_cols <- grep("\\.AVG_Beta$", colnames(beta.m), value = TRUE)
## Extract internal sample IDs by removing ".AVG_Beta".
## Example:
##   "1018133.AVG_Beta" becomes "1018133"
beta_sample_id <- sub("\\.AVG_Beta$", "", beta_cols)
## Check beta columns
head(beta_sample_id)
############################################################
## Module 5. Build mapping table from internal sample IDs to GSM IDs
############################################################

## GEO titles contain internal sample IDs in square brackets.
## Example:
##   title = "normal colon tissue from CRC patient [1018133]"
##   SampleID = "1018133"
sample_map <- data.frame(
  GSM = pD.all$geo_accession,
  title = pD.all$title,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
sample_map$SampleID <- sub(".*\\[([^]]+)\\].*", "\\1", sample_map$title)
head(sample_map)
## Match processed matrix sample IDs to GEO metadata sample IDs.
idx <- match(beta_sample_id, sample_map$SampleID)

beta_to_GSM <- data.frame(
  beta_column = beta_cols,
  SampleID = beta_sample_id,
  GSM = sample_map$GSM[idx],
  title = sample_map$title[idx],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

head(beta_to_GSM)
#View(beta_to_GSM)
## Check unmatched samples and NA value
sum(is.na(beta_to_GSM$GSM))
beta_to_GSM[is.na(beta_to_GSM$GSM), ]
############################################################
## Module 6. Manually fix known ID-format mismatches
############################################################

## In GSE199057, two technical replicates use hyphens in the processed matrix:
##   T1003588-rep1
##   T1003588-rep2
## But GEO metadata title uses spaces:
##   T1003588 rep1
##   T1003588 rep2
## Therefore, manually assign their GSM IDs.
sample_map[grepl("T1003588", sample_map$title, ignore.case = TRUE),
                       c("GSM", "SampleID", "title")]
beta_to_GSM$GSM[beta_to_GSM$SampleID == "T1003588-rep1"] <- "GSM5963372"
beta_to_GSM$title[beta_to_GSM$SampleID == "T1003588-rep1"] <- 
  "colon tumor sample from CRC patient [T1003588 rep1]"

beta_to_GSM$GSM[beta_to_GSM$SampleID == "T1003588-rep2"] <- "GSM5963373"
beta_to_GSM$title[beta_to_GSM$SampleID == "T1003588-rep2"] <- 
  "colon tumor sample from CRC patient [T1003588 rep2]"
## Recheck unmatched samples after manual correction.
sum(is.na(beta_to_GSM$GSM))

beta_to_GSM[is.na(beta_to_GSM$GSM), ]

############################################################
## Module 7. Remove plate-control samples from beta mapping table
############################################################

## Plate-control samples are technical controls.
## They should not be included in Tumor vs NAT / healthy_colon analysis.
beta_to_GSM_clean <- beta_to_GSM[
  !grepl("Plate-Control", beta_to_GSM$SampleID, ignore.case = TRUE),
]
## Confirm that all retained samples have GSM IDs.
sum(is.na(beta_to_GSM_clean))

############################################################
## Module 8. Generate strict beta matrix
############################################################

## Extract only AVG_Beta columns and convert to a numeric matrix.

beta <- as.matrix(beta.m[, beta_to_GSM_clean$beta_column, drop = FALSE])
mode(beta) <- "numeric"
rownames(beta) <- beta.m$TargetID## Set CpG probe IDs as row names.
colnames(beta) <- beta_to_GSM_clean$GSM## Set GSM IDs as column names.

## Check beta matrix.
## Expected:
##   rows = CpG probes
##   columns = biological GSM samples
##   values = beta values between 0 and 1
dim(beta)#224 samples
beta.m1 <- beta
############################################################
## Module 8. Align pD with beta.m1
############################################################

## Find samples present in pd but not in beta.m1
samples_to_remove <- setdiff(pd$ID, colnames(beta.m1)); samples_to_remove
length(samples_to_remove)

## Check the metadata of these samples
pd[pd$ID %in% samples_to_remove, ]

## Remove these samples from pd
pd <- pd[!pd$ID %in% samples_to_remove, , drop = FALSE]

## Reorder pd to match beta.m1 column order
pd <- pd[match(colnames(beta.m1), pd$ID), , drop = FALSE]

## Set row names as GSM IDs
rownames(pd) <- pd$ID

## Final checks
setequal(colnames(beta.m1), pd$ID)
identical(colnames(beta.m1), pd$ID)
identical(colnames(beta.m1), rownames(pd))

dim(beta.m1)
dim(pd)
table(pd$group)

# Set group order
pd$group <- factor(
  pd$group,
  levels = c("Normal","Tumor")
)

# Sort pd by group
sample_order <- order(pd$group)

pd <- pd[sample_order, , drop = FALSE]

# Set rownames as GSM ID
rownames(pd) <- pd$ID

# Reorder beta matrix columns according to pD
beta.m1 <- beta.m1[, rownames(pd), drop = FALSE]

# Final check
identical(rownames(pd), colnames(beta.m1))
table(pd$group)
pd1 <- pd
############################################################
## Module 9. Run ChAMP filtering
############################################################

## Run ChAMP filtering using the cleaned beta matrix and aligned pd.
myLoad <- champ.filter(beta = beta.m1, pd = pd1, arraytype = "EPIC")
## Save the ChAMP object for downstream analysis.
save(myLoad,file = 'step0.1-GSE199057_output.Rdata')

# For all downstream analyses, simply use this myLoad variable
#champ.filter() performs initial quality control:
#(1).Filters out probes with low detection p-value.
#(2).Removes probes with missing values.
#(3).Filters sex chromosomes or SNP-associated probes if needed.
#(4).The output myLoad is an object used for all downstream ChAMP analyses 
##(normalization, DMP, DMR, pathway analysis).
```

### GSE149282
```{r GSE149282}
## Module 0. Clear environment and load required packages
rm(list = ls())
options(stringsAsFactors = F)
require(GEOquery)
require(Biobase)
library("impute")
library(ChAMP)

############################################################
## Module 1. Read GEO metadata
#Ensure that the methylation beta matrix and the phenotype information strictly correspond to each other
## Download/read GEO series matrix metadata
eset <- getGEO("GSE149282",destdir = './',AnnotGPL = T,getGPL = F)
beta.m <- exprs(eset[[1]])
class(beta.m)
dim(beta.m)

############################################################
## Module 2. Build phenotype table pD
## Build a clean phenotype table.
## Modify this part when applying the pipeline to another dataset
pD.all <- pData(eset[[1]])
pD <- pD.all[,c(1,2)]
names(pD) <- c("group", "ID")
pD$group <- ifelse(grepl("normal",pD$group,ignore.case = T),
  "Normal","Tumor")

#⭐Check if the extracted groupings are correct
check_df <- data.frame(Title = pD.all$title, Group = pD$group)
head(check_df, 10)
tail(check_df,10)
table(pD$group)


############################################################
## Module 3. Align pD with beta.m
############################################################
## Check whether beta.m and pD contain the same sample set.
## setequal() ignores order.
identical(rownames(pD),colnames(beta.m))##This must be TRUE before champ.filter().
setequal(colnames(beta.m), pD$ID)
# Confirm that there are no duplicated sample names
sum(duplicated(colnames(beta.m)))
sum(duplicated(pD$ID))

# Reorder pD to match the column names of beta.m1
pD <- pD[match(colnames(beta.m), pD$ID), , drop = FALSE]

# Set pD row names as GSM IDs.
rownames(pD) <- pD$ID
## Final strict alignment check.
dim(beta.m)
dim(pD)

# Set group order
pD$group <- factor(
  pD$group,
  levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))

# Sort pD by group
sample_order <- order(pD$group)

pD <- pD[sample_order, , drop = FALSE]

# Reorder beta matrix columns according to pD
beta.m <- beta.m[, rownames(pD), drop = FALSE]

############################################################
## Module 4. Run ChAMP filtering
############################################################

## Run ChAMP filtering using the cleaned beta matrix and aligned pD.
myLoad <- champ.filter(beta = beta.m, pd = pD, arraytype = "EPIC")
## Save the ChAMP object for downstream analysis.
save(myLoad,file = 'step0.2-GSE149282_output.Rdata')
```

### GSE119526
```{r GSE119526}
rm(list = ls())
options(stringsAsFactors = F)

require(GEOquery)
require(Biobase)
library("impute")
library(ChAMP)

# Make sure that the methylation beta matrix and phenotype information correspond one-to-one.
eset <- getGEO("GSE119526", destdir = './', AnnotGPL = T, getGPL = F)
beta.m <- exprs(eset[[1]])
dim(beta.m)

# Prepare clinical information; note that each specific project may require adjustments.
pD.all <- pData(eset[[1]])
pD <- pD.all[, c(1, 2)]
names(pD) <- c("group", "ID")
pD$group <- ifelse(grepl("normal", pD$group, ignore.case = T), "Normal", "Tumor")
# Check if the grouping is correct.
check_df <- data.frame(Title = pD.all$title, Group = pD$group)
head(check_df)
# head(check_df, 10)
table(pD$group)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sort samples: normal first, CRC second
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# Check whether pD and beta.m contain the same samples
setequal(colnames(beta.m), pD$ID)

# Set group order: Normal first, Tumor second
pD$group <- factor(pD$group, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))

# Get sorted sample order
sample_order <- order(pD$group)

# Sort phenotype table
pD <- pD[sample_order, , drop = FALSE]

# Reorder beta matrix columns according to sorted pD
beta.m <- beta.m[, pD$ID, drop = FALSE]

# Final check: must be TRUE
identical(colnames(beta.m), pD$ID)

# Check group order
table(pD$group)

myLoad <- champ.filter(beta = beta.m, pd = pD, arraytype = "EPIC")
#save as .Rdata
save(myLoad, file = 'step0.3-GSE119526_output.Rdata')

```


## data combining

```{r }
## OVERALL STRATEGY IMPLEMENTATION
# GSE199057 → limma
# GSE149282 → limma
# GSE119526 → limma
##extract:Δβ + SE + P--CpG meta-analysis--DMR calling from meta CpG signal

rm(list = ls())
options(timeout = 100000) 
options(scipen = 20)

library(data.table)
library(limma)
library(metafor)

load("step0.1-GSE199057_output.Rdata")
g_GSE199057 <- myLoad$beta
g_GSE199057_group <- myLoad$pd
rm(myLoad)
g1 <- g_GSE199057
pd1 <- g_GSE199057_group

load("step0.2-GSE149282_output.Rdata")
g_GSE149282 <- myLoad$beta
g_GSE149282_group <- myLoad$pd
rm(myLoad)
g2 <-g_GSE149282
pd2 <- g_GSE149282_group

load("step0.3-GSE119526_output.Rdata")
g_GSE119526 <- myLoad$beta
g_GSE119526_group <- myLoad$pd
rm(myLoad)
g3 <-g_GSE119526
pd3 <- g_GSE119526_group

dim(g1)
dim(g2)
dim(g3)

dim(pd1)
dim(pd2)
dim(pd3)

colnames(pd1)
colnames(pd2)
colnames(pd3)

head(pd1);tail(pd1)
head(pd2);tail(pd2)
head(pd3);tail(pd3)

# unify CpG space
common_cpg <- Reduce(intersect, list(
    rownames(g1),
    rownames(g2),
    rownames(g3)
  ))

g1 <- g1[common_cpg, ]
g2 <- g2[common_cpg, ]
g3 <- g3[common_cpg, ]

all(colnames(g1) == pd1$ID)
all(colnames(g2) == pd2$ID)
all(colnames(g3) == pd3$ID)

# build phenotype
group1 <- factor(pd1$group, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))
group2 <- factor(pd2$group, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))
group3 <- factor(pd3$group, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))

## LIMMA per dataset

run_limma <- function(beta, group){
  
  beta <- as.matrix(beta)
  
  # Fixed effect direction: Tumor - Normal
  group <- factor(group, levels = c("Normal", "Tumor"))
  
  stopifnot(ncol(beta) == length(group))
  stopifnot(!any(is.na(group)))
  
  # Prevent beta values of 0 or 1 from producing infinite M values
  beta <- pmin(pmax(beta, 1e-6), 1 - 1e-6)
  
  # Convert beta values to M values
  mval <- log2(beta / (1 - beta))
  
  # Use a no-intercept design to explicitly define Tumor - Normal
  design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + group)
  colnames(design) <- levels(group)
  
  contrast.matrix <- makeContrasts(
    Tumor_vs_Normal = Tumor - Normal,
    levels = design
  )
  
  fit <- lmFit(mval, design)
  fit <- contrasts.fit(fit, contrast.matrix)
  fit <- eBayes(fit)
  
  res <- topTable(
    fit,
    coef = "Tumor_vs_Normal",
    number = Inf,
    sort.by = "none"
  )
  
  # CpG ID
  res$CpG <- rownames(res)
  
  # Manually calculate standard error
  se_vec <- sqrt(fit$s2.post) * fit$stdev.unscaled[, "Tumor_vs_Normal"]
  names(se_vec) <- rownames(fit$coefficients)
  res$SE <- se_vec[res$CpG]
  
  # Retain delta beta for biological interpretation
  res$deltaBeta <- rowMeans(beta[, group == "Tumor", drop = FALSE]) -
    rowMeans(beta[, group == "Normal", drop = FALSE])
  
  return(res)
}
res1 <- run_limma(g1, group1)
res2 <- run_limma(g2, group2)
res3 <- run_limma(g3, group3)

# CpG meta-analysis
meta_df <- data.frame(
  CpG = res1$CpG,
  
  beta1 = res1$logFC,
  se1   = res1$SE,
  
  beta2 = res2$logFC,
  se2   = res2$SE,
  
  beta3 = res3$logFC,
  se3   = res3$SE
)
## meta-analysis per CpG
## fixed-effect meta-analysis per CpG

b1 <- meta_df$beta1
b2 <- meta_df$beta2
b3 <- meta_df$beta3

s1 <- meta_df$se1
s2 <- meta_df$se2
s3 <- meta_df$se3

## inverse-variance weights
w1 <- 1 / s1^2
w2 <- 1 / s2^2
w3 <- 1 / s3^2

## fixed-effect pooled beta
beta_meta <- (w1 * b1 + w2 * b2 + w3 * b3) / (w1 + w2 + w3)

## pooled standard error
se_meta <- sqrt(1 / (w1 + w2 + w3))

## z statistic
z_meta <- beta_meta / se_meta

## two-sided p value
p_meta <- 2 * pnorm(abs(z_meta), lower.tail = FALSE)

## output
meta_res_fixed <- data.frame(
  CpG = meta_df$CpG,
  beta = beta_meta,
  se = se_meta,
  z = z_meta,
  p = p_meta
)

## FDR correction
meta_res_fixed$FDR <- p.adjust(meta_res_fixed$p, method = "BH")

############################################################
## DMR calling 
############################################################
## Custom meta-DMR calling from meta CpG signal
## Criteria:
## 1. CpG-level meta FDR < 0.05
## 2. Adjacent significant CpGs within 1000 bp are merged
## 3. DMR CpGs > 5
## 4. DMR mean Δβ > 0.2

library(data.table)
library(minfi)
library(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

## 1. Annotation

ann <- getAnnotation(IlluminaHumanMethylationEPICanno.ilm10b4.hg19)

ann_df <- data.frame(
  CpG = rownames(ann),
  chr = as.character(ann$chr),
  pos = as.integer(ann$pos),
  gene = as.character(ann$UCSC_RefGene_Name),
  relation_to_island = as.character(ann$Relation_to_Island),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

## 2. Prepare mean delta beta
## deltaBeta direction: Tumor - Normal

delta_df <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "CpG", all = FALSE), list(
  data.frame(CpG = res1$CpG, deltaBeta1 = res1$deltaBeta),
  data.frame(CpG = res2$CpG, deltaBeta2 = res2$deltaBeta),
  data.frame(CpG = res3$CpG, deltaBeta3 = res3$deltaBeta)
))

delta_df$mean_deltaBeta <- rowMeans(
  delta_df[, c("deltaBeta1", "deltaBeta2", "deltaBeta3")],
  na.rm = TRUE
)

## 3. Merge meta result + annotation + delta beta

dat <- merge(meta_res_fixed, ann_df, by = "CpG")
dat <- merge(dat, delta_df[, c("CpG", "mean_deltaBeta")], by = "CpG")

dat <- dat[
  is.finite(dat$beta) &
    is.finite(dat$se) &
    is.finite(dat$z) &
    is.finite(dat$p) &
    is.finite(dat$FDR) &
    is.finite(dat$mean_deltaBeta) &
    !is.na(dat$chr) &
    !is.na(dat$pos),
]

dat <- dat[dat$chr %in% paste0("chr", 1:22), ]

dat <- as.data.table(dat)

## 4. Define significant CpGs for DMR seed
## Hyper-DMR: Tumor - Normal > 0
sig <- dat[
  FDR < 0.05 &
    mean_deltaBeta > 0
]

sig[, chr_order := match(chr, paste0("chr", 1:22))]
setorder(sig, chr_order, pos)

## 5. Merge nearby significant CpGs into regions
## gap <= 1000 bp belongs to the same region
max_gap <- 1000

sig[, gap := pos - data.table::shift(pos, n = 1L, type = "lag"), by = chr]
sig[, new_region := is.na(gap) | gap > max_gap, by = chr]
sig[, region_id := cumsum(new_region)]

## 6. Summarize candidate DMRs
dmr_custom <- sig[
  ,
  .(
    chr = chr[1],
    start = min(pos),
    end = max(pos),
    width = max(pos) - min(pos) + 1,
    no.cpgs = .N,
    mean_deltaBeta = mean(mean_deltaBeta, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_deltaBeta = max(mean_deltaBeta, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_deltaBeta = min(mean_deltaBeta, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_FDR = min(FDR, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_FDR = mean(FDR, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_p = min(p, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_z = mean(z, na.rm = TRUE),
    CpGs = paste(CpG, collapse = ";"),
    genes = paste(
      base::unique(
        base::unlist(
          base::strsplit(paste(gene, collapse = ";"), ";")
        )
      ),
      collapse = ";"
    ),
    relation_to_island = paste(base::unique(relation_to_island), collapse = ";")
  ),
  by = region_id
]

## 7. Final filter: CpGs > 5 and mean Δβ > 0.2
dmr_hyper_custom <- dmr_custom[
  no.cpgs > 5 &
    mean_deltaBeta > 0.2
]

setorder(dmr_hyper_custom, min_FDR, -mean_deltaBeta)

dmr_hyper_custom
save(dmr_hyper_custom,file = "dmr_hyper_custom.Rdata")
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
